2606.28537.title: MammoFlow: Multiview Mammogram Synthesis with Anatomically Consistent Flow Matching
2606.28537.paper_summary: Multiview mammography relies on paired craniocaudal (CC) and mediolateral oblique (MLO) views to provide complementary projections of a 3D breast volume, enabling precise anomaly localization. However, acquiring high-quality, balanced datasets remains challenging for deep learning applications. We propose a novel method to synthesize multiview mammograms by leveraging the inherent geometric relationship between CC and MLO views. To enforce an implicit 3D consistency prior during generation, we develop an alignment module that searches a 2D affine transformation subspace to establish optimal anatomical correspondence. Leveraging this alignment, we introduce a pixel-space self-consistency loss based on the Earth Mover's Distance (EMD) between the 1D anteroposterior (AP) axis tissue distributions of the generated images. Integrated into a pretrained flow matching model, MammoFlow forces synthesized pairs to share physically plausible tissue distributions from the chest wall to the nipple. To our knowledge, this is the first work to guide multiview mammogram generation using implicit geometric tissue correspondence. Our method demonstrates superior image quality, passes expert radiologist evaluation, and generates physically consistent pairs that improve downstream classification AUC by 5%. Code is available at https://github.com/XYPB/MammoFlow
2606.28537.paper_summary_zh: 多視角乳房攝影依賴配對的頭尾（CC）和內外側斜（MLO）視圖來提供三維乳房體積的互補投影，使得精確的異常定位成為可能。<lb/>然而，為深度學習應用獲取高質量、平衡的數據集仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>我們提出了一種新方法，通過利用CC和MLO視圖之間固有的幾何關係來合成多視角乳房攝影圖像。<lb/>為了在生成過程中強制執行隱式三維一致性先驗，我們開發了一個對齊模塊，該模塊在2D仿射變換子空間中搜索，以建立最佳的解剖對應關係。<lb/>利用這一對齊，我們引入了一種基於地球搬運者距離（EMD）的像素空間自一致性損失，該損失基於生成圖像的1D前後（AP）軸組織分佈。<lb/>集成到預訓練的流匹配模型MammoFlow中，強制合成對共享從胸壁到乳頭的物理上合理的組織分佈。<lb/>據我們所知，這是第一個利用隱式幾何組織對應來指導多視角乳房攝影生成的工作。<lb/>我們的方法展示了優越的圖像質量，通過專家放射科醫生的評估，並生成物理上一致的對，從而使下游分類的AUC提高了5%。<lb/>代碼可在 https://github.com/XYPB/MammoFlow 獲得。
2606.28537.author: Yuexi Du et.al.
2606.28537.authors: Yuexi Du, Leya Barrientos, Laura Sheiman, John Lewin, Hemant D. Tagare, Nicha C. Dvornek
